label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The user provided a story and a question about the writer's opinion. The story is about a guy who is terminally ill with cancer and goes to a music store daily to buy CDs. He gets a slip from the girl every time, but he never asks her out. Eventually, he dies, and his mother finds all those slips with the same message. The question asks which of the four options is the writer's opinion. The options are (A) Don't wait to show your feelings, (B) Life always goes like that, (C) There is only one chance for everyone, (D) Show your feelings in a proper way. First, I need to understand the main message of the story. The key takeaway is that the guy didn't express his feelings to the girl, even though he had the chance every day. The twist at the end is that all the CDs had the same message from the girl, suggesting she might have felt the same way but never knew because he didn't act. The last sentence says, "That's the way life is. Don't wait to show those special people the way you feel. Tomorrow would be too late." So the main message here is about not delaying expressing your feelings. The story emphasizes that waiting might lead to missed opportunities. Looking at the options, (A) "Don't wait to show your feelings" directly aligns with the ending message. Option B, "Life always goes like that," is a bit vague. The story uses this example to make a point about not waiting, not just stating that life is like that in general. Option C is similar to A but phrased as "only one chance," which is close but not as precise. The story is more about not waiting rather
